sections.paragraph:
pl. עַקְרַבִּים m.-

(1) a scorpion, Eze 2:6. (Arab. عَقْرَ بُ id. To this corresponds the Greek σκόρπιος, the breathing being changed into sibilant; compare עָקַל σκόλιος.) It appears to be blended from עָקַר, عقر to wound, and עָקֵב the heel. See also מַעֲלֵה־עַקְרַבִּים

(2) a kind of scourge, furnished with sharp points, 1Ki 12:11, 14 1Ki 12:14 2Ch 10:11, 14 2Ch 10:14. So in Lat. scorpio, according to Isidore (Origg. v. 27), is virga nodosa et aculeata.
